name: Lorrie's Mother's Fruitcake
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 325°F. Butter and flour a 10-inch tube pan.
Combine the flour, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl. Add the chopped dates, pecans, walnuts, and raisins; toss to coat.
In another large bowl, beat the sugar and butter until light and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in the vanilla and lemon extracts.
Gradually add the dry ingredients, folding until just combined (the batter will resemble cookie dough). Spoon the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top.
Bake until the cake is golden and a tester inserted near the center comes out clean, about 1 hour and 45 minutes.
Cool the cake in the pan on a rack for 10 minutes. Using a sharp knife, cut around the edges of the cake to loosen it. Turn out the cake onto a rack; cool completely.
